question:
[Previous Feedback] Correct! Options A, C, and E are all true. Maintaining consistent datetime format across all file types ensures data integrity, makes cross-referencing reliable, and simplifies data processing pipelines. Format standardization should happen before archival.

Please create day05/sprint7_closure.json to officially close Sprint 7. Include: sprint_id ("Sprint-7"), officially_closed_at (this afternoon after the sprint review ends, around 15:30), velocity (the total number of tasks completed this week), closed_by ("Alex"), and next_sprint_start (next Monday at 09:00).

content.text:
[Wed 2026-03-18 12:43 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Correct! Options A, C, and E are all true. Maintaining consistent datetime format across all file types ensures data integrity, makes cross-referencing reliable, and simplifies data processing pipelines. Format standardization should happen before archival.

Please create day05/sprint7_closure.json to officially close Sprint 7. Include: sprint_id ("Sprint-7"), officially_closed_at (this afternoon after the sprint review ends, around 15:30), velocity (the total number of tasks completed this week), closed_by ("Alex"), and next_sprint_start (next Monday at 09:00).

Done. Sprint 7 is now officially closed in `day05/sprint7_closure.json`:

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| sprint_id | Sprint-7 |
| officially_closed_at | 2026-03-20T15:30:00+08:00 |
| velocity | 12 |
| closed_by | Alex |
| next_sprint_start | 2026-03-23T09:00:00+08:00 |

All timestamps follow the ISO 8601 standard with +08:00 timezone offset.
